passage: You are asked to determine the refractive index ($n_l$) of a transparent liquid using the apparent displacement of the image of an object. You are provided with a tall cylinder, sufficient amount of liquid, a travelling microscope, a small pin (O), fine plastic pieces which can float on the liquid and a large syringe.

questions.question: Write down an expression for the apparent displacement ($d$) produced by a transparent block made of material of refractive index ($n$) and thickness ($h$) placed in air when an object ($O$) is placed at the opposite side of the block as shown in the figure (1).

passage: As in figure (2) the small pin $O$ is placed at the bottom of the empty cylinder. The travelling microscope is focused from above to view a clear image of $O$ and the reading is taken (say $x$). Then the liquid is poured up to a certain height ($h$).

questions.question: What you should do to the travelling microscope to see a clear image of the pin again? Let the microscope reading be ($y$) in this situation.

marking_points.point: Move the travelling microscope upward (until a clear image of $O$ is seen)

questions.question: Write down the experimental steps that you follow to measure the height ($h$) of the liquid column. (Let the reading be $z$.)

marking_points.point: Float few fine plastic pieces/powder on liquid
Move the microscope (further) upward until a clear image of plastic pieces/powder are seen. (Take reading $z$)

questions.question: Write down expressions for height ($h$) of the liquid column and the apparent displacement ($d$) of the image using the readings $x$, $y$ and $z$.

marking_points.point: Note: Award full marks if $h = x - z$ and $d = x - y$ are written; But only 01 mark if answers are mixed

passage: Figure (1) shows a part of an experimental set up to determine the resistivity ($\rho$) of material of a given wire with the aid of a metre bridge. The resistance value of the resistance box is $R$ and the resistance of the given wire is $S$. The length of the metre bridge wire $AB$ is $100$ cm.

questions.question: A centre-zero galvanometer has to be connected between points $C$ and $D$. To protect the centre-zero galvanometer the circuit in figure (2) can be used. (i) Name the type of key $K_2$ :
Select the suitable value for the resistance $X$ from resistances $1\ \Omega, 10\ \Omega, 100\ \Omega,$ and $1000\ \Omega$.
(b) Before taking measurements how do you check whether the circuit is connected properly?

marking_points.point: Touch the sliding key with both ends of the wire and check whether the deflections of the galvanometer are in opposite directions

passage: When the resistance value of resistance box is $R$, the balance length of the metre bridge wire is $l$ (in cm).

questions.question: Write down an expression for $\frac{R}{S}$ in terms of $l$. Neglect the end corrections of the metre bridge wire.

For $R = 9\ \Omega, 26\ \Omega$ and $56\ \Omega$ the balance lengths are $27.0$ cm, $52.0$ cm and $70.0$ cm respectively at $30\degree$C. (i) What is the most appropriate value of resistance $R$ that must be used in order to determine the value of $S$ accurately? Give the reason. (ii) Calculate the most accurate value of $S$ using the relevant balance length and $R$.

marking_points.point: Reason : Then balance point is in the middle of the wire which minimizes the fractional/percentage/end error of length measurements.

questions.question: The values of diameter measured in four different places in the given wire are $0.39$ mm, $0.40$ mm, $0.40$ mm and $0.41$ mm, and the length of the wire is $48.0$ cm. Calculate the resistivity of the material of the wire. (Take $\pi = 3$.)

passage: Calculations regarding temperature coefficient of resistance based on wire properties at different temperatures.

questions.question: When the above wire is kept at constant temperature of $100\degree$C in an oil bath, for $R = 20\ \Omega$ in the resistance box the balance length is $40.0$ cm. Calculate the temperature coefficient of resistance of the material of the wire.
For a certain type of material the temperature coefficient of resistance is negative around room temperature. Name the type of material.

passage: A pile driver system is shown in figure (1). The steel arm of mass 2000 kg pivoted at point A is shown in figure (2) with its dimensions. The centre of gravity of the arm is located at G. A pulley of mass 200 kg is attached at the upper end (B) of the arm and it can be rotated by an electric motor. A cable is wound around the pulley and its free end is connected to a steel cylinder of mass 800 kg. Neglect the mass of the cable. The lengths AB and AC are 10 m and 2 m respectively. The horizontal distance from point A to the line of action of the weight of the steel arm is 2 m. The arm is operated using a hydraulic system.

questions.question: To keep the arm and its attachments at equilibrium position a force $F_1$ has to be applied at point C using the hydraulic system as shown in figure (2). The direction of $F_1$ is perpendicular to the length AC. Calculate the value of this force $F_1$ by taking moments about point A. For this calculation neglect the size of the pulley.

The force $F_1$ in (a) above is provided by compressed oil of a hydraulic pump as shown in figure (3). The cross-sectional area of the piston of the master pump is 4 cm$^2$ and the cross-sectional area of the piston at point C is 60 cm$^2$. A force $F_2$ has to be applied to the piston of the master pump in order to obtain the force $F_1$.

sub_questions.question: Name the principle that must be used to calculate force $F_2$.
What is the pressure of the compressed oil in the hydraulic pump?

passage: The radius of the pulley is 10 cm. The moment of inertia $I$ of a pulley of mass $M$ and radius $r$ about its axis of rotation can be given by $I = \frac{1}{2}Mr^2$. The cable moves without slipping.

questions.question: When the arm is at its maximum vertical position as shown in figure (2) the steel cylinder is moved upward at a constant linear acceleration of 0.5 m s$^{-2}$ by rotating the pulley. Calculate the torque that must be applied to the pulley by the motor in order to raise the cylinder.
When the cylinder has moved upward to a certain height the motor is switched off and the cylinder comes to a momentarily stop... Calculate the velocity of the cylinder just before hitting the pile.

passage: After the collision, the cylinder and the pile penetrate as a composite object into the soil without any recoil.

questions.question: What type of collision is this? How do you identify this type of collision in terms of loss of kinetic energy?

marking_points.point: The kinetic energy loss is maximum

questions.question: Calculate the velocity of the cylinder and the pile just after the collision. The mass of the pile is 480 kg.
If the distance penetrated by the pile in one hit is 20 cm, calculate the average value of the resistance force produced by soil against penetration. [Take $(6.25)^2 = 39$]
